narrative_votes_with_evidence.evidence_quote: Los Angeles Mayor Eric Garcetti joined a “global coalition of mayors” at a summit on climate change this week in Buenos Aires, Argentina, burning fossil fuels on an international flight rather than attending remotely and attending to pressing issues at home.
narrative_votes_with_evidence.reasoning: The text questions the effectiveness and logic of Garcetti's climate action by highlighting the contradiction of burning fossil fuels to attend a climate summit and suggesting he should have attended remotely or focused on local problems.

narrative_votes_with_evidence.narrative_name: CC: Criticism of institutions and authorities
narrative_votes_with_evidence.evidence_quote: Garcetti joined a U.S. Department of Transportation presentation on “high-quality urban transportation infrastructure,” though L.A.’s infrastructure is not widely considered “high-quality.”
narrative_votes_with_evidence.reasoning: This statement directly challenges the competence and integrity of Mayor Garcetti and the institutions he represents by pointing out a perceived hypocrisy between his participation in a presentation and the poor state of his city's infrastructure.

narrative_votes_with_evidence.evidence_quote: White Garcetti was away, Los Angeles continued to struggle with the high crime and homelessness that have come to define his legacy, as well as a crisis in the City Council triggered by racist comments from City Council members within Garcetti’s party.
narrative_votes_with_evidence.reasoning: The text criticizes the climate movement's priorities by juxtaposing Garcetti's international climate activism with severe, unresolved domestic crises, implying the movement and its leaders are out of touch with pressing local issues.

narrative_votes_with_evidence.evidence_quote: Los Angeles Mayor Eric Garcetti joined a “global coalition of mayors” at a summit on climate change this week in Buenos Aires, Argentina, burning fossil fuels on an international flight rather than attending remotely and attending to pressing issues at home.
narrative_votes_with_evidence.reasoning: The text questions the effectiveness and logic of Garcetti's climate action by highlighting the contradiction of burning fossil fuels to attend a climate summit, thereby criticizing the practical implementation of climate policies.

narrative_votes_with_evidence.narrative_name: CC: Criticism of institutions and authorities

narrative_votes_with_evidence.reasoning: This statement directly challenges the competence and credibility of both Mayor Garcetti and the U.S. Department of Transportation by contrasting their claims with the perceived reality of Los Angeles' infrastructure problems.

narrative_votes_with_evidence.evidence_quote: White Garcetti was away, Los Angeles continued to struggle with the high crime and homelessness that have come to define his legacy, as well as a crisis in the City Council triggered by racist comments from City Council members within Garcetti’s party.
narrative_votes_with_evidence.reasoning: The text criticizes the climate movement's priorities by juxtaposing Garcetti's international climate engagement with pressing domestic social crises, implying the movement and its leaders are out of touch with immediate local needs.

narrative_votes_with_evidence.evidence_quote: Los Angeles Mayor Eric Garcetti joined a “global coalition of mayors” at a summit on climate change this week in Buenos Aires, Argentina, burning fossil fuels on an international flight rather than attending remotely and attending to pressing issues at home.
narrative_votes_with_evidence.reasoning: The quote directly questions the effectiveness and logic of Garcetti's climate action by highlighting the contradiction of burning fossil fuels to attend a climate summit, thereby criticizing the practical implementation of climate policies.

narrative_votes_with_evidence.evidence_quote: White Garcetti was away, Los Angeles continued to struggle with the high crime and homelessness that have come to define his legacy, as well as a crisis in the City Council triggered by racist comments from City Council members within Garcetti’s party.

narrative_votes_with_evidence.evidence_quote: Garcetti joined a U.S. Department of Transportation presentation on “high-quality urban transportation infrastructure,” though L.A.’s infrastructure is not widely considered “high-quality.”
narrative_votes_with_evidence.reasoning: This quote criticizes the climate movement and its associated events by pointing out a perceived hypocrisy or disconnect between the ideals promoted at the summit (high-quality infrastructure) and the reality in Garcetti's own city, undermining the movement's credibility and impact.
